intent: MediPulse Connect is a unified hospital orchestration platform designed to eliminate fragmentation between patient care, administrative scheduling, and clinical data retrieval. Its core value proposition is the 'Live-Path' integration, which syncs patient telemetry with real-time electronic health records (EHR). The workflow enables patients to self-triage via secure symptom logging, triggers automated physician assignment based on specialization availability, and provides clinicians with a unified dashboard to manage vitals, medication adherence, and discharge summaries in a single interface.

entities.name: PatientClinicalProfile
entities.description: A FHIR-compliant resource aggregate storing validated identity data linked to government health IDs and current longitudinal clinical state.

entities.name: LivePathTelemetryStream
entities.description: A high-velocity buffer managing raw IoT sensor data incoming from medical hardware middleware, normalized for real-time analysis.

entities.name: TriageAssessmentKernel
entities.description: The logic engine that processes self-reported symptom logging against clinical decision support protocols to generate risk-weighted urgency scores.

entities.name: PhysicianAvailabilityMatrix
entities.description: A dynamic state machine tracking active staff shifts, specialization capacity, and current patient load to optimize automated resource allocation.

entities.name: MedicationAdherenceLedger
entities.description: An immutable record-keeping component that tracks medication administration, adherence patterns, and compliance alerts synced with the EHR.

entities.name: DischargeWorkflowOrchestrator
entities.description: A state-based controller that aggregates clinical summaries, pharmacy sign-offs, and follow-up scheduling requirements to automate the patient transition process.

architecture.description: The system architecture employs an event-driven microservices mesh where the PatientClinicalProfile acts as the source of truth. Mobile-originated symptom data enters the TriageAssessmentKernel, which triggers an event to the PhysicianAvailabilityMatrix to assign providers via an asynchronous task bus. Simultaneously, raw data from IoT medical hardware is ingested via a middleware bridge into the LivePathTelemetryStream, which continuously updates the clinical dashboard. Clinicians interact with a unified interface that pulls data from the MedicationAdherenceLedger and EHR-compliant backends. All state changes—from triage urgency to discharge readiness—are published as domain events, ensuring that the DischargeWorkflowOrchestrator can autonomously aggregate necessary artifacts to finalize patient departure while maintaining full FHIR interoperability.

endpoints.summary: Generate urgency score from symptom log
Query current staff shifts and capacity
Update discharge workflow status and summaries

rules.description: Ensure PatientClinicalProfile identity integrity against government health IDs
rules.logic: When a new profile is created or updated, validate the provided Government Health ID against a verified jurisdictional identity service before allowing data persistence.
If TriageAssessmentKernel generates a risk score exceeding the established clinical threshold, trigger an immediate notification to the PhysicianAvailabilityMatrix to bypass standard queues.

All incoming sensor data must be verified against current device calibration profiles; if telemetry variance exceeds defined physiological norms, flag as alert-worthy and update PatientClinicalProfile state.

An assignment to a PhysicianAvailabilityMatrix entity is only valid if the physician's current capacity is below 90% and they hold the appropriate specialty credential mapped to the patient's triage profile.
If a discrepancy between the MedicationAdherenceLedger and scheduled EHR medication orders is detected, trigger a non-compliance notification for review by the attending physician.
The DischargeWorkflowOrchestrator may only transition to 'ReadyForDischarge' if all clinical summaries, pharmacy sign-offs, and follow-up appointments are marked as completed in the source services.
